name: Savory Egg Custard with Clams and Shrimp
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons Chopped Chinese garlic, chives, or green onions
recipeInstructions:
This steamed egg dish exemplifies the simplicity of Chinese home cooking; it takes about 5 minutes to prepare. Use a heat-proof porcelain shallow bowl that is 7 inches wide by 3 inches deep with sloping sides for steaming or a 1-quart ceramic souffle dish or Pyrex bowl works just as well. To ensure a smooth, silky texture, avoid high heat when steaming.
1. Combine the clam juice, chicken broth, oil, wine, soy sauce, sugar, and pepper in a saucepan; bring to a boil.
2. Remove from heat; cool completely.
3. Lightly beat the eggs in a bowl. Slowly stir the cooled broth mixture into the eggs until thoroughly mixed. Avoid over-beating.
4. Scatter the clams and 2 tablespoons of the chives in the bottom of a 1-quart heat-proof bowl. Strain the egg mixture through a fine-mesh sieve over the clams.
5. Bring the water in a steamer to a boil over high heat. Place the bowl on a rack above the boiling water. Cover, reduce heat to a gentle boil, and steam for 2 minutes, or until the eggs begin to set.
6. Carefully remove cover from steamer; scatter the shrimp on top of the custard. Cover and steam for 5 minutes longer. The custard is done when a knife inserted into the center comes out clean.
7. Carefully remove bowl from the steamer. Drizzle with oyster sauce and garnish with remaining chives. Spoon over rice. Serve hot.
Serves 4 to 6. Per serving: 90 calories, 10 g protein, 2 g carbohydrate, 5 g fat (1 g saturated), 160 mg cholesterol, 186 mg sodium, 0 g fiber.
